what_it_does: A SessionStart hook that reads JSON from stdin, writes a PID-keyed self-marker file with session metadata, and best-effort backfills matching fabric channel registrations, failing silently on every error.
observations: Failure-isolated: all exceptions are caught and the script exits 0.
Marker is keyed by the claude-ancestor PID to avoid cwd collisions.
Marker write is atomic via a temp file and os.replace.
Imports _claude_ancestor_pid and SELF_MARKER_DIR from runtime.session_scan with a sys.path insert and a fallback if import fails.
Also scans .data/channels/*.json to set session_id and transcript_path on the registration whose claude_pid matches.
Does not mutate environment or edit ~/.claude.json.
standouts: Dual write: legacy self-marker plus fabric channel registration backfill.
Completely silent failure semantics for a global hook.
conventions: Atomic file writes with temp file and os.replace.
Docstring explains hook context and failure requirements.
concerns: Broad Exception swallowing can hide real bugs.
Imports a private underscore function from another module and mutates sys.path.
Scans and rewrites channel JSON files without file locking, risking races between concurrent sessions.
Opens channel files without a with statement for json.load.
No cleanup logic for stale marker files.
notes: Silent, PID-keyed session marker writer with a best-effort channel backfill; every failure is swallowed because it runs in a global SessionStart hook.
questions: What is the exact stdin JSON schema provided by Claude Code?
How does _claude_ancestor_pid remain stable across process trees?
What cleans up old or stale self-marker files?
What ordering guarantees exist between this hook and the .mjs announce hook that writes channel registrations?
Could concurrent sessions race when rewriting the same channel registration file?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches the stated purpose: it writes a PID-keyed self-marker and best-effort backfills channel registrations, remaining failure-isolated.
intention_signals: Provide a reliable, non-disruptive way for a session to record its own identity keyed by a stable ancestor PID, and to repair the richer fabric registration when the announce hook left session fields empty.
novelty: Using the claude-ancestor PID as the session-unique, cwd-independent self key, plus a hook-level backfill of a separate registration store that the announce hook cannot populate.
contribution: A failure-isolated, atomic marker writer that records session metadata keyed by ancestor PID and backfills channel registration session fields, giving the runtime an unambiguous self-id without editing ~/.claude.json.
summary_for_embedding: SessionStart hook that atomically writes a PID-keyed self-marker JSON with session_id, transcript_path, cwd, and timestamp, and best-effort backfills matching fabric channel registrations, failing silently.
intention_for_embedding: Enable unambiguous self-session identification across co-located sessions by recording hook-provided metadata under a stable ancestor PID and repairing the fabric registration store.

symbols.does: Writes the session self-marker and backfills matching channel registrations.
symbols.description: Reads Claude Code hook JSON from stdin, resolves the claude-ancestor PID, atomically writes a JSON marker keyed by that PID, then scans .data/channels/*.json to set session_id and transcript_path on the matching registration. Returns 0 and swallows all exceptions so the global SessionStart hook never fails.
